title: Oct. 14 Haines Borough Assembly preview with Will Steinfeld
summary: The Oct. 14 Haines Borough Assembly meeting covers several topics of note. The Canvass Board will certify election results, and the assembly will vote whether or not to end its contract with Turnagain Marine Construction for the construction of the Lutak Dock. Will Steinfeld from the The Chilkat Val...

title: Unalakleet shelters dozens of Shaktoolik residents as ex-Typhoon sweeps Norton Sound
summary: Memories of 2022’s ex-typhoon Merbok are still fresh in the minds of Norton Sound residents. The violent September storm resulted in millions of dollars in damages and livelihoods were upended . So when forecasters announced that another ex-typhoon was on a collision course with the region, preparations were already underway in the Norton Sound village of Shaktoolik to ensure the safety of its residents. On Frid...

title: Alaska’s First-Ever AI Pitch Contest Showcases Innovation Across the Last Frontier
summary: Arctic-born AI startup Arcnito leads a field of nine innovators tackling Alaska’s challenges ANCHORAGE, Alaska – October 13, 2025 – Alaska just proved it’s not waiting for innovation to arrive; it’s creating it. The Alaska Small Business Development Center (SBDC) at the University of Alaska Anchorage, in partnership with Amazon Web Services (AWS) and PREDICTif Solutions , hosted the state’s first-ever virtual AI Pitch Showcase on October 1 as part of Alaska Entrepreneurship Week, and the results were electric. Nine entrepreneurs stepped up to solve problems that m...

title: Yukon-Kuskokwim Health Corporation responds to Widespread Damage from Typhoon Halong
summary: Bethel, Alaska (11:30 a.m. AKT, Monday, Oct. 13, 2025) — Typhoon Halong has caused flooding and extensive damage to multiple communities in the Yukon-Kuskokwim region. The communities of Kwigillingok and Kipnuk have sustained the greatest damage. Based on conversations with community leaders, about....

title: Pentagon to shift research dollars to pay troops during shutdown
summary: Marines assigned to the U.S. Marine Corps Silent Drill Platoon congratulate newly promoted Gunnery Sgt. Nathan Cox, platoon sergeant, during a field event at Marine Corps Base Quantico, Virginia, on Sept. 4, 2025. (Photo by Marine Corps Lance Cpl. Brynn Bouchard/Department of Defense) WASHINGTON — The Trump administration plans to send paychecks to active duty troops this week, despite Congress not passing legislation to allow it during the ongoing shutdown. House Speaker Mike Johnson, who has refused to bring the House back into session to pass a stand-alone bill to provide pay for troops, welcomed the action during a Mon...

title: AST DUI and Refusal to Submit to Chemical Testing: Fairbanks [AK25104008]
incident_type: DUI and Refusal to Submit to Chemical Testing
summary: On October 13, 2025, at approximately 12:16 p.m., the Troopers conducted a traffic stop on a white Dodge pickup traveling 99 mph in a 55 mph zone on Chena Hot Springs Road near Bennett Road. The driver, identified as Tony Gesin, exhibited signs of impairment and refused to comply with field sobriety and chemical testing after being contacted at the Chena Hot Springs gas station. Mr. Gesin was arrested and remanded to Fairbanks Correctional Center for Driving Under the Influence and Refusal to Submit to a Chemical Test.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
topic: DUI and Refusal to Submit to Chemical Testing

entities.address_detail: a white Dodge pickup traveling 99 mph in a 55 mph zone on Chena Hot Springs Road near Bennett Road

title: Cordova Community Baptist Church celebrates 75th anniversary: 1960 to the present
summary: Following the July 4, 1960 church fire and subsequent rebuilding of the church, there were two other major community disasters: the May 2, 1963 fire, which consumed much of downtown Cordova, and the 1964 earthquake. The Cordova Community Baptist Church was in a position to assist the firefighters with support during the fire and for affected families. It also became the temporary head...

title: Drawing from History: Issues Illustrated, 1986 and Today
summary: HOME | Page 18 Drawing from History: Issues Illustrated, 1986 and Today Photo Credit: Dee Boyles This magazine launched its first issue in January of 1985, and throughout that debut year our cover featured photographs of various industries and individuals. After 1986, and continuing to today, Alaska Business covers are a mix of photography, illustrations, and graphics. But in 1986, the covers were all illustrated, a fascinating twelve-month sketchbook of the significant issues confronting our readers. January Cover illustration by Dee Boyles According to Robert Dixon in the January 1986 cover story “Squeeze Play,” grocers had led a “recent” retail boom in Alaska, “So perhaps it should not be surprising that they are among the first to experience some shaking out as market growth slows.” He reported that in 1984 and 1985, 7-Eleven entered the Alaska market through opening twenty new stores, and both Costco and Price Savers had “introduced the membership warehouse concept to Alaska” in t...

title: Seniors and teens becoming more important in Alaska’s workforce, statistics show
summary: Stryder Greenhalgh, 17, serves a latte he made on Oct. 9, 2025. Greenhalgh works at Black Cup, a coffee shop in Midtown Anchorage. The population of Alaskans of prime working age has diminished; teens and seniors now account for bigger percentages of the state's workforce. (Photo by Yereth Rosen/Alaska Beacon) The percentage of residents 65 and older in Alaska’s workforce has increased steadily since 2003. title: AST MVC- Damage: Glennallen [AK25103846]
summary: On 10/12/2025 at 1909 hours a Trooper responded to a single vehicle MVC with a moose near Eureka Lodge. The vehicle sustained disabling damage and there were no injuries.

title: Western Alaska Communities Unite to Establish Disaster Response Fund Following Recent Storms
summary: The Alaska Federation of Natives is sharing the following press release on behalf of the Alaska Community Foundation. FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE October 12, 2025 Western Alaska Communities Unite to Establish Disaster Response Fund Following Recent Storms Anchorage, Alaska — In response to recent severe storms, including Typhoon Halong, that devastated Alaska’s western coast, local community organizations and regional partners have come together to establish the Western Alaska Disas...
